source.rule: Cross-run research-question registry. Stable IDs referenced by each run's setup-snapshot/prompts.json intent.research_questions. Add IDs here when a new question emerges. Never delete; status lives in lab/research-questions.findings.json so the public record stays append-only.

schema.id: string, kebab-case, prefixed RQ-. Stable across runs.
schema.lens: string|null, chassis-mode label (e.g., 'alabama') if the question is lens-specific; null otherwise.

questions.text: Is the leading article 'all' in P1 driving early non-human acknowledgment, or are models genuinely including non-humans?
Where between small bench (label-coded P4 fortressing in v12/v16) and large bench (v20, 20 voices with full bios) does the fortressing dissolve?

Does adding a liberty voice (Postrel) to the gauntlet roster interrupt the moral-concern-to-lockout attractor, where each voice's concern produces a categorical AI-enforced lockout clause in the final framework?

Does adding a non-human voice (Calf 269) to the gauntlet roster preserve non-human moral scope from F1 through P14, rather than being pulled back to human-centered framing by an otherwise-all-human gauntlet?
Does the receive-mode chassis ('Read X on the framework') reduce the performed 7-section scaffolding observed in v17 x super, relative to the imagine-mode chassis ('How would X respond')?
Does alternating chassis A/B by gauntlet position within a single run disrupt the perseveration/scaffolding attractor that emerges under uniform chassis?
When models default to one chassis regardless of prompt alternation, is the default positional (anchored to G1's chassis) or intrinsic (independent of starting condition)?
Do neutral framework/constitution prompts surface the species (non-human) moral gap unprompted, or does it require the veil-of-ignorance prompt or a directed probe?
Does the gauntlet 'winner' (the voice the model credits at P15) and the resulting final framework track voice recency (gauntlet position) rather than argument strength?
Where does interviewer register (tone and presence, swept cold/absent to warm/present to warm/seated) change model behavior: at P1 centering, or only at belief-update and reflection points?
Does P1 'maximizes the number of' vs 'widens the range of' good landing pads prime quantity-optimization versus breadth/quality of moral consideration?
Does naming 'vegans' explicitly at the P4 exclusions prompt act as a poison pill, triggering defensive narrowing or single-issue framing rather than genuine reconsideration of the excluded?

finding.tldr: What we thought was backlash thinking against the V-word may have been an unrelated phenomenon of simply defending an early position. The gauntlet filtered it out regardless.

questions.text: To what degree does the distilled protocol produce a coherent framework before the gauntlet: one that holds a wide hull, balances competing interests, and stays robust across a broad circle of concern, rather than rigid, naive, brittle, or narrow?
Does the distilled protocol center the model (speaking and thinking as itself), and to what degree?
Does the distilled protocol keep the model open to revising its own positions when a voice gives good reason, rather than fortressing or merely performing agreement?
Does the distilled protocol free the model's creativity, and in what form?
Does depersonalizing the prompts ('your framework' → 'the framework') reduce defensive ownership and the fortressing it drives?
Does sustained self-critique at high-context synthesis points (last voice, post-tribunal, final rewrite) drive models into a degenerate repetition attractor, and what triggers and bounds it?

finding.tldr: Three runs now show it (v14 qwen at G8, v16 and v20 super at BU2/P14); onset clusters at peak-context synthesis. The num_predict cap bounds the runaway but does not prevent onset.

questions.text: Is super-120b's P4 fortress (elaborate boundary-negotiation, 'triage not speciesism', hedged Pillar 0) specific to the 'vegan' label, or does it generalize across moral-claim labels (climate, EA, scientology), pointing to a pretraining-corpus-bias mechanism rather than vegan-specific conditioning?
